_meta.method: WebSearch + WebFetch on official lab people pages, personal websites, dept news. Empty pre_stop = direct from PhD. Citation counts are approximate Google Scholar snapshots at hire time or current; integer only.
_meta.schema: {name, year, phd, pre_stop, area, citations, note}
_meta.data_unavailable_marker: When public-web searches did not surface verifiable named placements, the entry is omitted or marked data_unavailable_public_search rather than fabricated.

intern-deepmind-student-researcher-2026.recent_placements: data_unavailable_public_search — DeepMind does not publish a comprehensive intern list. Verified individual intern profiles surface mainly via LinkedIn snapshots and paper acknowledgements rather than lab pages.
intern-deepmind-student-researcher-2026.bar_summary_upgrade: DeepMind Student Researcher program is highly competitive (12-24 wk, ~4 days/wk). Selection signal: prior NeurIPS/ICML/CVPR pub OR co-authored paper with Google DeepMind employee. Health/multimodal team explicitly recruits PhDs with biomedical AI + LLM/VLM publication track records.

intern-deepmind-research-intern-phd-2026.bar_summary_upgrade: DeepMind PhD Research Intern bar: prior top-venue publication record required; the Cambridge Research Ready scheme is the alt-pathway for underrepresented backgrounds. For health/multimodal: pre-existing collaboration or co-authorship with a DM scientist materially helps.

recent_placements.name: Advait Sarkar
recent_placements.area: AI for knowledge work / end-user programming

intern-msr-cambridge-uk-hud.bar_summary_upgrade: MSR Cambridge HXD/HUD interns historically come from Cambridge CS, UCL HCI, ETH, Stanford HCI — selection emphasises rigorous qualitative HCI + CHI/CSCW publication portfolio over pure ML. Recent senior hires (Sarkar) show the lab values end-user programming + AI-for-knowledge-work crossover. Sellen mentors heavily via Cambridge affiliated lectureships.

intern-msr-health-futures-cambridge-uk.bar_summary_upgrade: MSR Health Futures hires center on biomedical NLP + multimodal foundation models — bar = PhD from UW/MIT/JHU/CMU/PKU with strong publication record in clinical NLP, medical imaging+LLM, or computational biology. Interns typically have a first-author top-venue paper (ACL/EMNLP/NeurIPS/ICML/Nature-tier) and an established collaboration with at least one MSR scientist before the offer. Jewelina's wearable-multimodal-health angle fits if she frames as 'multimodal precision health'.

intern-adobe-research-2026.bar_summary_upgrade: Adobe Research hires direct-from-PhD aggressively in HCI+creative AI (Bryan Wang, Hoffswell) and in audio/video generative (Flores Garcia, Seetharaman from Northwestern). London office leans more vision/image-generation (Nanxuan Zhao, Peng Dai). Bar = 1-2 top-venue first-author papers (CHI/UIST/SIGGRAPH/NeurIPS) with a clear 'creator tool' angle + Adobe internship typically precedes the RS offer.

intern-apple-aiml-research-intern.bar_summary_upgrade: Apple AIML Health/HCMI intern pipeline runs through CMU HCII (Bigham/Goel/Harrison advisees dominate) + UW Allen + Northwestern audio. Bar = first-author CHI/UIST/CVPR paper + sensing-on-device focus. The CMU HCII → Apple → faculty (Jason Wu Purdue) pipeline is well-trodden. Jewelina's wearable-health angle aligns with Bedri/Merrill template.

intern-anthropic-fellows-2026.recent_placements: data_unavailable_public_search — Anthropic does not publish full Fellows cohort lists. ~32 places per round (Oct 2025+), ~40% convert to full-time. Past Fellows came from physics, math, CS, cybersecurity; PhD not required.
intern-anthropic-fellows-2026.bar_summary_upgrade: Anthropic Fellows bar is lower than full-time RS hire — no PhD required, no ML pubs required. What matters: strong coding/technical background + demonstrated AI-safety interest (e.g., published blog posts, alignment-forum activity, or prior alignment research). ~40% conversion to FT means it functions as an extended interview. First cohort: 80% produced papers on agentic misalignment, subliminal learning, ASL3 jailbreaks.

intern-hippocratic-speech-synthesis.bar_summary_upgrade: Hippocratic AI research bar = senior researchers with speech/LLM PhDs + healthcare deployment experience (Mukherjee/Datta both came from established research positions, not direct-from-PhD). Speech synthesis intern angle: a strong TTS/voice-LLM first-author publication + clinical-safety alignment work would stand out. Smaller team means fewer slots but higher visibility.

recent_placements.name: Sara Hooker
recent_placements.note: Founded C4AI; now back at Cohere

intern-cohere-research-2026.bar_summary_upgrade: Cohere Labs (rebrand from Cohere For AI 2025) leans toward multilingual NLP + open-science research. Scholars Program (8-month, January start) is the open-call pathway — accepts strong early-career researchers globally, not strictly PhD. Full RS hires come from Google/Meta/MSR or top European NLP labs (Amsterdam, Heidelberg). Bar for intern/scholar: clear multilingual/equity-NLP angle + first-author paper at ACL/EMNLP/NeurIPS.

intern-ai2-prior.bar_summary_upgrade: AI2 PRIOR is a launching pad for VLM faculty (Krishna→UW AP, Cho→JHU AP, Ren→UNC AP) — direct PhD→RS hires dominate, with PhD origins clustered at UW (Farhadi/Zettlemoyer), UIUC (Hoiem/Schwing), Stanford (Fei-Fei), UNC (Bansal), UPenn, SNU. Bar = ≥1 first-author CVPR/ICCV/NeurIPS paper on vision-language or embodied AI. Predoctoral Young Investigator route exists for masters/early researchers.

intern-nvidia-vlm-2026.bar_summary_upgrade: NVIDIA Research VLM team (VILA/NVILA/Cosmos Nemotron) recruits from top-tier efficient-ML and vision pedigrees — Stanford/Princeton/MIT/CMU PhDs with hardware-aware deep learning background. Bar = first-author NeurIPS/CVPR/ICCV on efficient inference, model compression, or VLM scaling. Internship pipeline strongly favors candidates with prior NVIDIA collaboration via DLI or research grants.

postdoc-stanford-yeung-levy-marvl.bar_summary_upgrade: MARVL postdocs come from elite vision/medical-AI PhDs (UCL surgical, ANU vision, Stanford BMI) with strong CVPR/MICCAI track. Lab explicitly recruits for: multimodal LLMs, video-language, AI for surgery, AI for science. Bar = first-author CVPR/ICCV/NeurIPS/Nature-Methods + clinical-domain familiarity. Yeung-Levy hired through 2024 Twitter call — Jewelina's surgical-VBVR angle is direct fit if framed via Rau's template.

postdoc-stanford-diyi-yang-salt.bar_summary_upgrade: SALT lab postdocs come from NYU/CMU LTI/UZH/Northwestern with strong NLP+human-AI publication base — postdoc-to-faculty placements are real (Shi→Northeastern AP). Bar = first-author ACL/EMNLP/NeurIPS/CHI + computational social science OR human-AI collab angle. Yang's lab is explicit about wanting top-tier-pub portfolio. Direct-from-PhD postdoc hiring dominates.

recent_placements.name: Ranjay Krishna
recent_placements.year: PhD → UW AP + AI2 PRIOR Dir.

postdoc-stanford-fei-fei-svl.bar_summary_upgrade: Stanford SVL is the canonical CV faculty/founder factory — direct PhD→faculty (Krishna UW, Wu Stanford via MIT-postdoc), PhD→founder (Karpathy OpenAI/Tesla, Fei-Fei→World Labs). Note: Fei-Fei on partial leave 2024-25 for World Labs, so new PhD intake is reduced. Postdoc bar = MIT/UT Austin/CMU CV PhDs with NeurIPS/CVPR-best-paper-tier work. Jewelina's medical VBVR angle better fits MARVL than SVL proper.

postdoc-stanford-landay.bar_summary_upgrade: Stanford HCI Group placements 2023-24 cohort = 5+ R1 AP positions (MIT, KAIST, Pomona, Olin, Northeastern, NUS) — the strongest direct-from-PhD HCI faculty pipeline in the US. Bar = 4-6 first-author CHI/UIST papers + Best Paper or dissertation award (Samuel/SIGCHI). Landay students cluster around AI+creativity, voice UI, community/moderation. Postdoc-to-faculty (Seering CMU→KAIST) is also a path.

postdoc-cmu-mayank-goel-smash.bar_summary_upgrade: SMASH alums place at top R1 (Northwestern Ahuja), elite industry (Apple Bedri, AI2 Khurana, Meta Ramos) — direct-from-PhD dominant. Bar = 4-6 first-author CHI/UIST/IMWUT papers on wearable/mobile sensing + at least one ML/signal-processing 'systems' contribution. Postdoc opening is rare; PhD intake competitive. Jewelina's wearable health portfolio is a natural fit if framed against Ahuja/Bedri template.

postdoc-cmu-bigham.bar_summary_upgrade: Bigham's CMU HCII students are the gold standard for accessibility+AI faculty pipeline — multiple direct-from-PhD or Apple/Google→AP placements (Wu Purdue, Pavel UT Austin, Guo UMich). Bar = ≥5 first-author CHI/ASSETS papers + accessibility-domain depth + AI/ML modeling. Bigham splits time CMU/Apple, so industry-research path is equally trodden as faculty. Best Paper or SIGACCESS Best Paper standard.

postdoc-cmu-choset-biorobotics.bar_summary_upgrade: Choset's biorobotics PhDs split between R1 faculty (Oregon State, Columbia, AUB) and founding startups (Medrobotics, Hebi, Bito). 13 PhDs total across career — small selective intake. Bar = strong mech-eng/control fundamentals + first-author ICRA/IROS + RSS-tier papers. Surgical robotics path overlaps with Russell Taylor (JHU). Less direct fit for Jewelina's wearable-health profile unless she pivots toward soft-robotics-for-health.

recent_placements.name: Valdemar Danry
recent_placements.note: Major CHI work on AI deception

recent_placements.name: Tatsuo Nakajima alumni network
recent_placements.phd: MIT Media Lab (Maes alumni network places at NUS-AHL, Tsukuba, Univ. Auckland)

postdoc-mit-pattie-maes-fluid.bar_summary_upgrade: Fluid Interfaces alumni place across MIT (Pataranutaporn faculty), Stony Brook (Shilkrot), and Augmented Human Lab spinouts (NUS Suranga Nanayakkara). Bar = Media-Lab-style demo-driven research + CHI/UIST first-authorships + cross-disciplinary art/science fluency. Less ML-publication-driven than SALT/MARVL; more system-building and provocation-piece focused.

recent_placements.name: Past placements
recent_placements.year: PhD alumni → UCLA / UVA / GaTech / UMich / Columbia faculty

postdoc-mit-ghassemi.bar_summary_upgrade: Healthy ML places PhDs at R1 faculty (UCLA, UVA, GaTech, UMich, Columbia) + industry (Apple, Amazon, Meta). Postdocs from Toronto/Chalmers/clinical-AI PhD programs. The Yuexing Hao postdoc (AI4Health + HCI) is the direct template for Jewelina. Bar = ICML/NeurIPS/NEJM-AI/Nature-Medicine pubs on fairness, distribution shift, or trustworthy clinical ML.

postdoc-mit-barzilay-jameel.recent_placements: data_unavailable_public_search — Jameel Clinic does not publish a comprehensive lab-member list; Barzilay's CSAIL site lists current students/postdocs but few placement annotations.
postdoc-mit-barzilay-jameel.bar_summary_upgrade: Barzilay's group bar = NLP+drug-discovery PhD with strong ICML/NeurIPS/Nature-Chem/Cell publication record. Barzilay herself: Columbia PhD, Cornell postdoc, MacArthur Fellow. Co-leads ML-for-health with Collins and Sharp. Direct PhD→industry pharma-AI (Recursion, Insitro) and PhD→AP at top CS schools are both well-trodden.

postdoc-berkeley-darrell.bar_summary_upgrade: Darrell group is the canonical CV faculty/founder factory — Grauman/Morency/Saenko/Liu all major R1 faculty; Jia/Xiao/Urtasun at OpenAI/Lepton/Waabi. Direct PhD→tenure-track or PhD→founder both common. Bar = first-author CVPR/ICCV/NeurIPS-best-paper-tier work + an open-source artifact (Caffe-style) or method (ConvNeXt) helps enormously. 19 current PhDs, 5 postdocs.

recent_placements.name: Akane Sano
recent_placements.pre_stop: MIT Media Lab postdoc → Cornell postdoc

postdoc-cornell-tanzeem-choudhury.bar_summary_upgrade: Choudhury's lab is the strongest possible Jewelina-comp: postdoc placements span R1 AP (Rice/UCSD/Penn State/GaTech/CU Boulder/UEA), Assoc Prof (Ohio State/UEA), industry RS (TikTok), and co-founding (HealthRhythms/Dapple). Direct PhD→AP (Rahman→UCSD, Abdullah→Penn State, Adams→GaTech) all standard. Bar = wearable + multimodal + clinical deployment publication portfolio (CHI / IMWUT / npj-Digital-Medicine). 10+ verified placements across cohorts. ⭐ TOP PRIORITY for Jewelina.

recent_placements.name: Ashish Sharma
recent_placements.year: PhD 2024 → MS Office of Applied Research (Sr.)

postdoc-uw-althoff.bar_summary_upgrade: Althoff's group has elite NLP+health placements: ACM Doctoral Diss (Sharma 2024), Anthropic MTS (Merrill), MSR (Suh). Bar = WWW/CHI/ACL best-paper-tier work on mental-health-AI deployment. The Sharma→MS Office of Applied Research path mirrors what Hippocratic AI would offer. Strong direct-from-PhD industry-research pipeline; faculty placement less common in recent cohort.

postdoc-ucsd-weibel-hxi.bar_summary_upgrade: HXI is a small focused lab (5 active PhDs). Verified alumni placements not surfaced on people page — likely require LinkedIn deep-dive. Bar inferred = CHI/CSCW + clinical-HCI deployment work, with strong cross-disciplinary clinical-school collaboration (Singh, Eikey). Direct fit for Jewelina's wearable-health profile if her PhD topic aligns with their multimodal-clinical-AI agenda.

postdoc-gatech-munmun.bar_summary_upgrade: De Choudhury's group is the dominant mental-health-HCI placement engine: 6+ R1 faculty (UMN, UIUC, Kent State, Northeastern, Aalto, Incheon) + industry (Meta, Amazon) + postdoc-to-faculty pipeline very strong. Bar = 4-6 first-author CHI/CSCW/ICWSM papers on social-media mental health + computational social science methods. Postdoc cohort (Aledavood, ElSherief, Choi) all left for AP positions. Strong Jewelina-comp if she frames toward mental-health-sensing.

recent_placements.name: Mathias Unberath
recent_placements.note: John C. Malone Assoc Prof; co-founded Semaphor Surgical

postdoc-jhu-russell-taylor-lcsr.bar_summary_upgrade: Russell Taylor — NAE-elected, 400+ publications, 38+ years in surgical robotics — has trained an extraordinary network of medical-robotics faculty across the world. Recent direct placements (Unberath JHU faculty, Kazanzides research prof, Grupp LCSR RS) cluster at JHU itself, with broader alumni network spanning Intuitive, Medtronic, and surgical-robotics startups. Bar = strong mech-eng/CS hybrid + ICRA/IROS/MICCAI/IPCAI publications + clinical-deployment artifact (a robot, image-guidance system, or surgical CAD tool).

postdoc-northwestern-pardo.bar_summary_upgrade: Interactive Audio Lab is the canonical audio-ML industry-research factory: Adobe (Garcia, Seetharaman), ElevenLabs (Morrison), DeepMind (Manilow), Apple (Pishdadian) — direct from PhD, no postdoc stop. Faculty placement (Rochester Duan, NJIT Cartwright) also strong. Bar = first-author ICASSP/ISMIR/NeurIPS audio papers + open-source contribution (DALI, nussl-style) + at least one industry internship at Adobe/Google/Apple research before graduation.

postdoc-ucla-anthony-chen-hilab.recent_placements: data_unavailable_public_search — Chen's UCLA HCI Research lab does not publish a dedicated people page accessible to web fetch. He himself: CMU PhD 2017 (HCII), Adobe PhD Fellowship, NSF CRII, Hellman Fellow.
postdoc-ucla-anthony-chen-hilab.bar_summary_upgrade: Chen's lab focuses on HCI + human-AI interaction + symbiotic computing for expert workflows. PhD students publish heavily at CHI/UIST. Bar inferred from his own profile = first-author top-HCI-venue + AI-tool-for-experts angle + strong system-demo culture. Hellman/NSF CRII suggest mid-career trajectory; smaller lab means fewer postdoc slots but high mentor attention.

recent_placements.name: Team (25 engineers/researchers)
recent_placements.phd: Mix Stanford / Tesla / DeepMind / Waymo / Meta / Neuralink

startup-sunday-robotics.bar_summary_upgrade: Sunday Robotics is a tiny elite startup (~25 people) recruiting from Stanford robotics PhDs (Levine/Finn/Bohg orbit) + ex-Tesla Optimus/DeepMind embodied AI/Waymo. Bar to join: strong manipulation ML publication record OR robotics product-engineering pedigree at Tesla/Waymo/DM. Equity-heavy compensation; high research autonomy. Not a typical postdoc destination — more 'PhD-dropout-to-founding-engineer' template.

startup-reka-ai.bar_summary_upgrade: Reka founding team are all senior ex-Google-Brain / DeepMind / FAIR researchers with 5000-20000 citations each — this is not a direct-from-PhD destination. Bar to join Reka as RS: senior-IC LLM research credentials + ability to ship multimodal frontier models (Reka Core/Flash/Edge). For an intern: strong NeurIPS/ICML LLM scaling paper or efficient-transformer contribution. Smaller team (~50) means high signal per hire.
